title: tarenna sambucina var. tahitensis
intro: Tarenna sambucina var. tahitensis, also known as Tahitian Tarenna, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae and is native to the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is valued for its ability to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Tarenna sambucina var. tahitensis can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used as a topical application to aid in wound healing.
